hero.headline: UPES Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA with a Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation. NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, UGC-DEB approved. Logistics Management, Supply Chain Strategy, Procurement and Sourcing, Warehouse and Inventory Management, Demand Planning, Supply Chain Analytics, Global Logistics, and Sustainable SCM in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: UPES Online MBA Logistics and SCM: NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, fees Rs 1,95,000 (Year 1: Rs 95,000 / Year 2: Rs 1,00,000), zero-cost EMI at Rs 8,125/month. SCM depth with Logistics Management, Supply Chain Strategy, Procurement, Warehouse Management, Demand Planning, Supply Chain Analytics, Global Logistics, and Sustainable SCM.

about.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management is offered by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies, Dehradun. UPES holds NAAC A accreditation, NIRF #45 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #36 in Management (2025). The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Logistics and SCM specialisation delivers Sem 3-4 subjects covering the end-to-end supply chain management stack. Sem 3 covers Logistics Management and Transportation, Supply Chain Strategy and Network Design, Procurement and Strategic Sourcing, Warehouse and Inventory Management, and Demand Planning and Forecasting. Sem 4 covers Strategic Management, Supply Chain Analytics and Technology, Global Logistics and Trade Compliance, Sustainable Supply Chain Management, and Dissertation.

UPES carries a distinct competitive advantage in logistics and supply chain education given its heritage in petroleum, energy, and infrastructure sectors. These are industries with the most complex supply chains in India, covering multi-modal logistics, energy commodity transportation, port and terminal operations, and cross-border trade compliance. Logistics and SCM is priced at Rs 1,95,000, slightly above UPES's standard MBA tier, reflecting the sector-specialised curriculum and industry connect with logistics, port, and energy sector employers.

sem3Subjects.name: Logistics Management and Transportation
sem3Subjects.description: Design multi-modal transportation networks, manage fleet operations, and apply freight analytics to road, rail, air, and sea logistics optimisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Strategy and Network Design
sem3Subjects.description: Design end-to-end supply chain networks, evaluate make-or-buy decisions, and optimise node configurations for cost, service, and resilience.

sem3Subjects.name: Procurement and Strategic Sourcing
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic sourcing frameworks, manage vendor evaluation, negotiate supply agreements, and build supplier development programmes for procurement roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Warehouse and Inventory Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design warehouse layouts, apply WMS technology, manage ABC inventory classification, and optimise safety stock and reorder point calculations.

sem3Subjects.name: Demand Planning and Forecasting
sem3Subjects.description: Apply statistical forecasting models, manage S&OP processes, and use demand sensing tools to reduce supply chain variability and stockouts.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop supply chain-led competitive strategy, evaluate vertical integration decisions, and design logistics network investment frameworks for business growth.

sem4Subjects.name: Supply Chain Analytics and Technology
sem4Subjects.description: Apply supply chain analytics platforms, evaluate ERP and WMS technology, and implement IoT-based supply chain visibility for operations roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Logistics and Trade Compliance
sem4Subjects.description: Manage international freight, customs compliance, EXIM documentation, and global trade regulations for international supply chain operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Sustainable Supply Chain Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design green logistics programmes, manage carbon footprint tracking, and apply ESG frameworks to supply chain procurement and operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Dissertation
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied SCM research project on supply chain network optimisation, logistics technology adoption, or sustainable procurement strategy.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all UPES MBA specialisations. Core includes Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Marketing Management, Quantitative Techniques, Operations Management, OB and HRM, Project Management, Financial Management, Research Methodology, and Business Analytics for Managers.

whoHires.body:
Logistics and Supply Chain Management MBA graduates from UPES Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** E-commerce logistics, FMCG supply chain, Energy and petroleum logistics, Manufacturing supply chain, 3PL and freight forwarding, Port and terminal operations

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Executive, Procurement Executive, Warehouse Operations Executive, Demand Planning Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Warehouse Manager, S&OP Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of Supply Chain, VP Logistics, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director Procurement, VP Operations

**Top hiring organisations (UPES alumni placements):** TCS, Infosys, HCL, Wipro, Deloitte, KPMG, and logistics, FMCG, and energy sector companies. UPES's energy sector heritage gives SCM graduates direct pathways to supply chain roles at ONGC, NTPC, GAIL, IOCL, and energy infrastructure companies where petroleum and gas logistics are core operations.

skills.body:
The UPES Logistics and SCM specialisation builds skills that e-commerce, FMCG, and energy sector supply chain employers test at SCM manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Strategy and Network Design: end-to-end supply chain network modelling, node configuration optimisation, and make-or-buy analysis for SCM manager and consultant roles
- Procurement and Strategic Sourcing: strategic sourcing methodology, vendor evaluation frameworks, and supply agreement negotiation for procurement and strategic sourcing roles
- Warehouse and Inventory Management: WMS technology management, ABC inventory control, and safety stock calculation for warehouse and inventory manager roles
- Supply Chain Analytics and Technology: ERP, WMS, and IoT-based supply chain visibility platform management for supply chain technology and operations roles

**Applied skills:**
- Demand Planning and Forecasting: statistical forecasting model application, S&OP process management, and demand sensing for FMCG and manufacturing supply chain roles
- Global Logistics and Trade Compliance: international freight management, EXIM documentation, and customs compliance for global supply chain and trade operations roles

**Soft skills:** Presenting supply chain cost reduction proposals to operations leadership, managing third-party logistics partners, negotiating freight contracts, and communicating supply chain risk to boards.

comparisons.body:
Comparing UPES Online MBA Logistics and SCM against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Logistics and SCM:** Amity covers logistics, SCM, and supply chain strategy with strong all-India brand at Rs 2,25,000. UPES Logistics and SCM at Rs 1,95,000 adds dedicated Demand Planning and Forecasting and Sustainable Supply Chain Management subjects. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than Amity's management category performance. UPES's energy sector alumni network is a distinct advantage for petroleum and infrastructure logistics careers.

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics and SCM:** LPU Logistics and SCM carries NIRF #31 University ranking with supply chain and logistics subjects at Rs 1,61,600 with grant. UPES Logistics and SCM at Rs 1,95,000 includes dedicated Supply Chain Analytics and Sustainable SCM subjects alongside strong energy sector industry connect. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than LPU's management category standing.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Supply Chain Management:** NMIMS SCM carries strong Mumbai FMCG and 3PL brand recognition. For Mumbai-based consumer goods and retail supply chain careers, NMIMS is the stronger choice. UPES Logistics and SCM is better positioned for energy sector supply chain, North India FMCG logistics, and e-commerce fulfilment operations roles.

fees.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management has a total program fee of Rs 1,95,000 across 2 years (4 semesters).

**Year-wise fee schedule:**
- Year 1 (Semesters 1 and 2): Rs 95,000
- Year 2 (Semesters 3 and 4): Rs 1,00,000

**Zero-cost EMI (no-cost EMI available):**
- Rs 8,125 per month for 24 months
- Tenure options: 3 to 36 months
- Zero interest (0% cost EMI) through UPES NBFC lending tie-ups
- Contact our counsellor for current tenure options and approval terms

**Scholarships available (confirmed from UPES scholarships portal):**
- **Academic/Work Experience scholarship**: 10% for 3+ years experience with 55%+ graduation marks; 15% for 5+ years experience; 20% for 10+ years experience
- **Alumni scholarship**: 20% for UPES degree programme graduates
- **Vijay (Sports) scholarship**: 35% to 100% based on national/international achievement level
- Scholarships cannot be combined with corporate MOU discounts
- Scholarship benefit applied semester-wise; eligibility confirmed at admission

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with UPES each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) and carries the UPES name with the MBA designation. The Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. UPES is WES recognised, making the degree suitable for international credential evaluation.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from UPES Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Sandeep Yadav
items.liked: Supply Chain Analytics and Demand Planning subjects were directly applicable to my FMCG supply chain role. UPES alumni at IOCL and energy logistics companies gave me direct referrals for supply chain roles. NIRF #36 Management was a strong credential.
items.disliked: Global Logistics case studies were more focused on energy sector trade lanes than FMCG consumer goods logistics, which limited relevance for some classmates.
items.body: I had 6 years in logistics operations and the UPES SCM MBA gave me the management credential for a supply chain manager role. UPES's energy sector alumni network was genuinely differentiated from other online MBA SCM programmes.

items.name: Meena Rawat
items.liked: Procurement and Strategic Sourcing and Warehouse and Inventory Management subjects were well-structured. Sustainable SCM was a valuable addition given current ESG demands.
items.disliked: For Mumbai FMCG SCM careers, NMIMS carries stronger brand recognition. UPES is strongest in North India and energy sector logistics.
items.body: Good programme for North India manufacturing and energy logistics careers. UPES alumni network at Haridwar and Rishikesh industrial belt companies was active for supply chain placements.

items.name: Ajay Bisht
items.liked: Supply Chain Strategy and Network Design was strong. Global Logistics and Trade Compliance directly applicable to my EXIM coordination role.
items.disliked: Course fee at Rs 1,95,000 is slightly higher than some peer programmes. The premium is justified by energy sector alumni access but not ideal for all profiles.
items.body: Strong SCM credential for North India and energy sector logistics careers. WES recognition was helpful for a Canada immigration assessment. NIRF #36 Management ranking is a genuine differentiator.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for UPES Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management?
faqs.answer: The total program fee is Rs 1,95,000 across 2 years. Year 1 is Rs 95,000 and Year 2 is Rs 1,00,000. Zero-cost EMI is available at Rs 8,125 per month for 24 months. Reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before payment.

faqs.question: Why is UPES Logistics and SCM MBA priced higher than other UPES MBA specialisations?
faqs.answer: UPES Logistics and Supply Chain Management is priced at Rs 1,95,000 compared to Rs 1,75,000 for standard UPES MBA specialisations. The higher fee reflects the sector-specialised curriculum, industry-embedded faculty, and UPES's strong industry connect with petroleum logistics, port operations, and energy infrastructure companies.

faqs.question: Does UPES Logistics MBA cover Supply Chain Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. Supply Chain Analytics and Technology is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering ERP, WMS platforms, and IoT-based supply chain visibility tools. Combined with Demand Planning and Forecasting in Sem 3, graduates develop both quantitative and technology application skills for modern SCM analyst and manager roles.

faqs.question: What scholarships does UPES offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: UPES offers Academic/Work Experience scholarship (10% for 3+ years, 15% for 5+ years, 20% for 10+ years experience with 55%+ graduation), Alumni scholarship (20%), and Vijay Sports scholarship (35% to 100%). Eligibility confirmed at admission.

faqs.question: Is UPES Online MBA Logistics suitable for energy sector supply chain roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. UPES's 20+ year energy and petroleum heritage gives Logistics and SCM graduates direct alumni access at ONGC, NTPC, IOCL, GAIL, and energy infrastructure companies. The Global Logistics and Trade Compliance subject is directly applicable to petroleum logistics, LNG terminal operations, and energy commodity trade roles.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A, NIRF #36 Management logistics and SCM MBA with energy sector alumni networks at Rs 1,95,000.
You are targeting supply chain manager, procurement manager, or logistics operations roles in energy, FMCG, or e-commerce companies.
You want zero-cost EMI at Rs 8,125/month and WES-recognised degree credentials.
consider: If Mumbai FMCG or retail supply chain brand recognition is the priority, NMIMS Online MBA Supply Chain Management carries stronger Mumbai consumer goods company recognition.
If EXIM-focused international trade careers are the goal, UPES's own International Business specialisation or Amity International Business may cover EXIM law more directly.
If the budget is tighter, LPU Online MBA Logistics and SCM at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is a comparable alternative at a lower fee point.
